title: Linux kernel will support $ORIGIN, sort of
excerpt: For some reason, during TacoSprint 2026 I decided to see if we could tackle relocatable binaries in Nix.
content_html:
For some reason, during TacoSprint 2026 I decided to see if we could tackle relocatable binaries in Nix.

I enjoy these lofty goals to push Nix and the surrounding ecosystem forward. I am bold if not stupid.

I left the last earlier post with one potential idea of how to get there:

We could patch the Linux kernel so that $ORIGIN is supported in PT_INTERP and the shebang.

I waded through the complexity of sending patches over email (turns out I actually enjoy this workflow!), and sent a proposal to the Linux kernel mailing list.

My first attempt here proposed simply adding direct support for $ORIGIN in the Virtual File System (VFS) subsystem.

I waited nervously. I was expecting the result from what I had come to read about online; someone non-politely telling me to F$#CK OFF because there is something I missed, misunderstood or did not consider. 🤬

The result was completely different. 😲

Christian Brauner, the maintainer for VFS responded to me in good faith, asking for the rationale for the change and eventually proposing some ways in which such a support could make it into the subsystem.

Note It definitely helped having someone like John Ericson chime in and advocate why having a non-fixed interpreter (PT_INTERP) is useful to Nix and other use-cases (i.e. Buck & Bazel).

He offered that potentially we could leverage eBPF as a programmable way to select an interpreter through binfmt_misc.

Whoa! 🤯

I wanted to merely allow $ORIGIN but a programmable selection could let us do anything!

The idea must have really intrigued him because soon-after, on his vacation, Christian offered the first draft of such a solution. We went back and forth a little over the mailing list and the end result is a patch series that will make its way into -next branch in the near future.

If you don’t know what eBPF is or binfmt_misc, WTF did we just collaborate on?

Let’s take a look!

I won’t do eBPF justice, and there are plenty of articles online about it as it’s quite in-vogue at the moment.

tl;dr; You can write programs in a C subset that gets compiled to an instruction set whose virtual machine is running within the kernel. Shouldn’t the kernel be super fast? Yes, the programs are jitted to their native CPU architecture and the programs have a fixed-time slice. Isn’t this some crazy vulnerability for the kernel? Before any code is loaded it is “verified” to be safe. Checkout this guide for more info.

We can now support $ORIGIN with a relatively simple eBPF program:

SEC("struct_ops.s/match")
bool BPF_PROG(nix_match, struct linux_binprm *bprm)
{
  return !bpf_strncmp(bprm->buf, 4, "\x7f" "ELF");
}

SEC("struct_ops.s/load")
int BPF_PROG(nix_load, struct linux_binprm *bprm)
{
  char path[256];
  long n;

  n = bpf_path_d_path(&bprm->file->f_path, path, sizeof(path));
  if (n < 0)
    return n;

  /* derive the loader location from the binary's path */

  return bpf_binprm_set_interp(bprm, path, sizeof(path));
}

SEC(".struct_ops.link")
struct binfmt_misc_ops nix = {
  .match = (void *)nix_match,
  .load = (void *)nix_load,
  .name = "nix",
};

Once the above program is loaded and registered into the kernel, we then ask the binfmt_misc subsystem to trigger it. Checkout this thread if you want to see the complete example.

> bpftool struct_ops register nix_origin.bpf.o /sys/fs/bpf
> echo ':origin:B::::nix:' > /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/register

What does that mean?

It means that every binary now triggers the nix_match function above, in this case any ELF file, but it could be executables with a new segment like PT_INTERP_NIX, and the kernel will ask nix_load to determine the interpreter to use dynamically.

Our special BPF program has support for $ORIGIN 💥

What else could you do?

Well we can now even completely replace the traditional QEMU binfmt_misc registration script with a BPF program now like this one.

What else can we do?

Since we can now programmatically select our interpreter based on anything in the file, we can do quite a lot. I’m keen to hear your suggestions and ideas 💡.

Some of the smaller items are that we can even support $ORIGIN in the shebangs (#!$ORIGIN/bin/ld.so) very easily as seen here: we simply look at the first 256 bytes of the file and look for $ORIGIN to trigger.

One downside or side-effect of the traditional binfmt_misc hand-off was that the way in which the desired final binary was invoked was non-transparent.

The registered interpreter becomes the process. It owns the entire process identity, and the binary you actually asked to run gets demoted to an argument. For wine or qemu that’s acceptable as they are emulators but for a per-binary BPF loader that might pick a traditional ld.so it does not make much sense.

This leaks in a few painful ways but the simplest are :

  • argv[0] and /proc/<pid>/cmdline show the interpreter invocation, not what you executed.
  • /proc/self/exe names the interpreter. Relocatable programs commonly locate themselves through /proc/self/exe, and instead they find the dynamic linker. 😩

Christian sent a large patch series for this as well. His latest patch series adds two new dispatch modes that close the gap from opposite ends and covers a few other gotchas that these modes can fix.

The loader substitition L is the one I’m most excited about for Nix.

With the L flag, the kernel executes the matched binary natively as the main image, and merely substitutes the registered interpreter for the loader named in the binary’s PT_INTERP. binfmt_misc stops being a hand-off and becomes a plain PT_INTERP override. There’s no contract and no identity to reconstruct, so a stock dynamic loader works unchanged.

Where does this leaves us?

I’ll be tracking the Linux kernel releases and, once this lands in -next and ships in a tagged release, I plan to upstream a NixOS module that registers the $ORIGIN support at boot. 🎉

The plan is to gate it on a new PT_INTERP_NIX segment rather than matching every ELF file. That keeps things backwards compatible: the BPF handler only kicks in for binaries that explicitly opt-in by carrying the new segment. This means Nix produced binaries continue to work without the BFP handler but those that have it may elevate themselves to relocatable status.

A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for. — John A. Shedd

I am bold if not stupid. I left the last earlier post with one potential idea of how to get there: We could patch the Linux kernel so that $ORIGIN is supported in PT_INTERP and the shebang. I waded through the complexity of sending patches over email (turns out I actually enjoy this workflow!), and sent a proposal to the Linux kernel mailing list. My first attempt here proposed simply adding direct support for $ORIGIN in the Virtual File System (VFS) subsystem. I waited nervously. Note It definitely helped having someone like John Ericson chime in and advocate why having a non-fixed interpreter (PT_INTERP) is useful to Nix and other use-cases (i.e. Buck & Bazel). He offered that potentially we could leverage eBPF as a programmable way to select an interpreter through binfmt_misc. Whoa! 🤯 I wanted to merely allow $ORIGIN but a programmable selection could let us do anything! The idea must have really intrigued him because soon-after, on his vacation, Christian offered the first draft of such a solution. If you don’t know what eBPF is or binfmt_misc, WTF did we just collaborate on? Let’s take a look! It means that every binary now triggers the nix_match function above, in this case any ELF file, but it could be executables with a new segment like PT_INTERP_NIX, and the kernel will ask nix_load to determine the interpreter to use dynamically. Our special BPF program has support for $ORIGIN 💥 What else could you do? sentences.en: Shouldn’t the kernel be super fast?
sentences.zh: 内核不应该超级快吗？

sentences.en: Yes, the programs are jitted to their native CPU architecture and the programs have a fixed-time slice.
sentences.zh: 是的，程序会根据其本机 CPU 架构进行调整，并且程序具有固定的时间片。

sentences.en: Isn’t this some crazy vulnerability for the kernel?
sentences.zh: 这不是内核的一些疯狂漏洞吗？

sentences.en: Before any code is loaded it is “verified” to be safe.
sentences.zh: 在加载任何代码之前，都会“验证”其安全性。

sentences.en: Checkout this guide for more info.
sentences.zh: 查看本指南以获取更多信息。

blocks.html_en: The registered interpreter becomes the process. It owns the entire process identity, and the binary you actually asked to run gets demoted to an argument. sentences.en: argv[0] and /proc/<pid>/cmdline show the interpreter invocation, not what you executed.
sentences.zh: argv[0] 和 /proc/<pid>/cmdline 显示解释器调用，而不是您执行的内容。

blocks.html_en: • /proc/self/exe names the interpreter. Relocatable programs commonly locate themselves through /proc/self/exe, and instead they find the dynamic linker. 😩

sentences.en: /proc/self/exe names the interpreter.
sentences.zh: /proc/self/exe 命名解释器。

sentences.en: Relocatable programs commonly locate themselves through /proc/self/exe, and instead they find the dynamic linker. 😩
sentences.zh: 可重定位程序通常通过 /proc/self/exe 定位自己，而是找到动态链接器。 😩

blocks.html_en: Christian sent a large patch series for this as well. His latest patch series adds two new dispatch modes that close the gap from opposite ends and covers a few other gotchas that these modes can fix.

sentences.en: Christian sent a large patch series for this as well.
sentences.zh: Christian 也为此发送了一个大型补丁系列。

sentences.en: His latest patch series adds two new dispatch modes that close the gap from opposite ends and covers a few other gotchas that these modes can fix.
sentences.zh: 他的最新补丁系列添加了两种新的调度模式，可以缩小两端之间的差距，并涵盖这些模式可以修复的其他一些问题。

blocks.html_en: The loader substitition L is the one I’m most excited about for Nix.

sentences.en: The loader substitition L is the one I’m most excited about for Nix.
sentences.zh: 对于 Nix 来说，装载机替代品 L 是我最兴奋的一个。

blocks.html_en: With the L flag, the kernel executes the matched binary natively as the main image, and merely substitutes the registered interpreter for the loader named in the binary’s PT_INTERP. binfmt_misc stops being a hand-off and becomes a plain PT_INTERP override. There’s no contract and no identity to reconstruct, so a stock dynamic loader works unchanged.

sentences.en: With the L flag, the kernel executes the matched binary natively as the main image, and merely substitutes the registered interpreter for the loader named in the binary’s PT_INTERP.
sentences.zh: 使用 L 标志，内核将本地执行匹配的二进制文件作为主映像，并且仅用注册的解释器替换二进制文件的 PT_INTERP 中命名的加载程序。

sentences.en: binfmt_misc stops being a hand-off and becomes a plain PT_INTERP override.
sentences.zh: binfmt_misc 不再是交接，而是变成普通的 PT_INTERP 覆盖。

sentences.en: There’s no contract and no identity to reconstruct, so a stock dynamic loader works unchanged.
sentences.zh: 没有合同，也没有需要重建的身份，因此库存动态加载器的工作原理不变。

blocks.html_en: Where does this leaves us?

sentences.en: Where does this leaves us?
sentences.zh: 这给我们留下了什么？

blocks.html_en: I’ll be tracking the Linux kernel releases and, once this lands in -next and ships in a tagged release, I plan to upstream a NixOS module that registers the $ORIGIN support at boot. 🎉

sentences.en: I’ll be tracking the Linux kernel releases and, once this lands in -next and ships in a tagged release, I plan to upstream a NixOS module that registers the $ORIGIN support at boot. 🎉
sentences.zh: 我将跟踪 Linux 内核版本，一旦它进入 -next 并在标记版本中发布，我计划上游 NixOS 模块，该模块在启动时注册 $ORIGIN 支持。 🎉

blocks.html_en: The plan is to gate it on a new PT_INTERP_NIX segment rather than matching every ELF file. That keeps things backwards compatible: the BPF handler only kicks in for binaries that explicitly opt-in by carrying the new segment. This means Nix produced binaries continue to work without the BFP handler but those that have it may elevate themselves to relocatable status.

sentences.en: The plan is to gate it on a new PT_INTERP_NIX segment rather than matching every ELF file.
sentences.zh: 计划是将其门控在新的 PT_INTERP_NIX 段上，而不是匹配每个 ELF 文件。

sentences.en: That keeps things backwards compatible: the BPF handler only kicks in for binaries that explicitly opt-in by carrying the new segment.
sentences.zh: 这保持了向后兼容：BPF 处理程序仅针对通过携带新段显式选择加入的二进制文件启动。

sentences.en: This means Nix produced binaries continue to work without the BFP handler but those that have it may elevate themselves to relocatable status.
sentences.zh: 这意味着 Nix 生成的二进制文件可以在没有 BFP 处理程序的情况下继续工作，但那些拥有 BFP 处理程序的二进制文件可能会将自己提升到可重定位状态。

blocks.html_en: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for. — John A. Shedd

sentences.en: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for. — John A.
sentences.zh: 停泊在港口的船是安全的，但这并不是造船的目的。 — 约翰·A.

sentences.en: Shedd
sentences.zh: 谢德

blocks.html_en: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for. — John A. Shedd

sentences.en: A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for. — John A.
sentences.zh: 停泊在港口的船是安全的，但这并不是造船的目的。 — 约翰·A.

sentences.en: Shedd
sentences.zh: 谢德

content_html_zh:
出于某种原因，在 TacoSprint 2026 期间，我决定看看我们是否可以解决 Nix 中的可重定位二进制文​​件问题。

我喜欢这些推动 Nix 和周围生态系统向前发展的崇高目标。 我即使不愚蠢，也很大胆。

我在上一篇文章中留下了一个关于如何实现这一目标的潜在想法：

我们可以修补 Linux 内核，以便在 PT_INTERP 和 shebang 中支持 $ORIGIN。

我费力地解决了通过电子邮件发送补丁的复杂性（事实证明我真的很喜欢这个工作流程！），并向 Linux 内核邮件列表发送了一份提案。

我在这里的第一次尝试建议简单地在虚拟文件系统 (VFS) 子系统中添加对 $ORIGIN 的直接支持。

我紧张地等待着。 我期待着我在网上读到的内容的结果；但事实并非如此。有人不礼貌地告诉我滚蛋，因为我错过了、误解了或没有考虑到一些事情。 🤬

结果完全不同。 😲

VFS 的维护者 Christian Brauner 真诚地回复了我，询问更改的理由，并最终提出了一些将这种支持纳入子系统的方法。

注意：约翰·埃里克森（John Ericson）这样的人插话并提倡为什么非固定解释器（PT_INTERP）对 Nix 和其他用例（即 Buck 和 Bazel）有用，这绝对有帮助。

他提出我们有可能利用 eBPF 作为一种可编程方式来通过 binfmt_misc 选择解释器。

Whoa! 🤯

我只想允许 $ORIGIN 但可编程选择可以让我们做任何事情！

这个想法肯定引起了他的兴趣，因为不久之后，在他的假期中，克里斯蒂安提出了这样一个解决方案的初稿。 我们在邮件列表上来回了一些，最终的结果是一个补丁系列，它将在不久的将来进入 -next 分支。

如果你不知道 eBPF 或 binfmt_misc 是什么，那我们刚才合作的到底是什么？

我们来看看吧！

我不会公正地评价 eBPF，网上有很多关于它的文章，因为它目前非常流行。

太长；博士；您可以在 C 子集中编写程序，该程序会被编译为虚拟机在内核中运行的指令集。 内核不应该超级快吗？ 是的，程序会根据其本机 CPU 架构进行调整，并且程序具有固定的时间片。 这不是内核的一些疯狂漏洞吗？ 在加载任何代码之前，都会“验证”其安全性。 查看本指南以获取更多信息。

我们现在可以使用一个相对简单的 eBPF 程序来支持 $ORIGIN：

SEC("struct_ops.s/match")
bool BPF_PROG(nix_match, struct linux_binprm *bprm)
{
  return !bpf_strncmp(bprm->buf, 4, "\x7f" "ELF");
}

SEC("struct_ops.s/load")
int BPF_PROG(nix_load, struct linux_binprm *bprm)
{
  char path[256];
  long n;

  n = bpf_path_d_path(&bprm->file->f_path, path, sizeof(path));
  if (n < 0)
    return n;

  /* derive the loader location from the binary's path */

  return bpf_binprm_set_interp(bprm, path, sizeof(path));
}

SEC(".struct_ops.link")
struct binfmt_misc_ops nix = {
  .match = (void *)nix_match,
  .load = (void *)nix_load,
  .name = "nix",
};

一旦上述程序被加载并注册到内核中，我们就会要求 binfmt_misc 子系统触发它。 如果您想查看完整的示例，请查看此线程。

> bpftool struct_ops register nix_origin.bpf.o /sys/fs/bpf
> echo ':origin:B::::nix:' > /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/register

这意味着什么？

这意味着现在每个二进制文件都会触发上面的 nix_match 函数，在本例中是任何 ELF 文件，但它可以使用像 PT_INTERP_NIX 这样的新段来执行，并且内核将要求 nix_load 确定要动态使用的解释器。

我们的特殊 BPF 计划支持 $ORIGIN 💥

你还能做什么？

好吧，我们现在甚至可以用像这样的 BPF 程序完全替换传统的 QEMU binfmt_misc 注册脚本。

我们还能做什么？

由于我们现在可以根据文件中的任何内容以编程方式选择解释器，因此我们可以做很多事情。 我很想听到您的建议和想法💡。

一些较小的项目是我们甚至可以非常轻松地在 shebangs (#!$ORIGIN/bin/ld.so) 中支持 $ORIGIN，如下所示：我们只需查看文件的前 256 个字节并查找要触发的 $ORIGIN。

传统 binfmt_misc 切换的一个缺点或副作用是调用所需的最终二进制文件的方式是不透明的。

注册口译员成为流程。 它拥有整个进程标识，并且您实际要求运行的二进制文件被降级为参数。 对于 wine 或 qemu 来说这是可以接受的，因为它们是模拟器，但对于可能选择传统 ld 的二进制 BPF 加载器来说，这没有多大意义。

这会以一些痛苦的方式泄漏，但最简单的是：

  • argv[0] 和 /proc/<pid>/cmdline 显示解释器调用，而不是您执行的内容。
  • /proc/self/exe 命名解释器。 可重定位程序通常通过 /proc/self/exe 定位自己，而是找到动态链接器。 😩

Christian 也为此发送了一个大型补丁系列。 他的最新补丁系列添加了两种新的调度模式，可以缩小两端之间的差距，并涵盖这些模式可以修复的其他一些问题。

对于 Nix 来说，装载机替代品 L 是我最兴奋的一个。

使用 L 标志，内核将本地执行匹配的二进制文件作为主映像，并且仅用注册的解释器替换二进制文件的 PT_INTERP 中命名的加载程序。 binfmt_misc 不再是交接，而是变成普通的 PT_INTERP 覆盖。 没有合同，也没有需要重建的身份，因此库存动态加载器的工作原理不变。

这给我们留下了什么？

我将跟踪 Linux 内核版本，一旦它进入 -next 并在标记版本中发布，我计划上游 NixOS 模块，该模块在启动时注册 $ORIGIN 支持。 🎉

计划是将其门控在新的 PT_INTERP_NIX 段上，而不是匹配每个 ELF 文件。 这保持了向后兼容：BPF 处理程序仅针对通过携带新段显式选择加入的二进制文件启动。 这意味着 Nix 生成的二进制文件可以在没有 BFP 处理程序的情况下继续工作，但那些拥有 BFP 处理程序的二进制文件可能会将自己提升到可重定位状态。

停泊在港口的船是安全的，但这并不是造船的目的。 — 约翰·A. 谢德
